Findings

BUG-01 HIGH

Kebab menu does not close on ESC, outside-click, or toggle

Surface/assets/ (asset detail page)
Caught byTC_BH_13_KebabMenuClosesOnOutsideClick

Description

The asset detail page kebab menu (Edit Asset / Delete Asset options) cannot be dismissed by any standard mechanism. This is a focus trap and a violation of the WAI-ARIA menu pattern.

Steps to reproduce

  1. Navigate to /assets and log in.
  2. Click any row in the assets grid to open the detail page.
  3. Click the three-dot kebab icon (top-right of the detail header).
  4. Verify the menu opens with "Edit Asset" and "Delete Asset" items.
  5. Press the ESC key.
  6. Click on an empty area of the page (e.g., the page header).
  7. Click the kebab icon again (toggle attempt).

Expected behavior

Per WAI-ARIA menu pattern: ESC should close the menu. Per MUI default ClickAwayListener: outside clicks should close the menu. Toggle (re-clicking the trigger) should also close the menu.

Actual behavior

Menu remains open and visible after ALL three close methods. The user is stuck — the only way to dismiss is to navigate away or click an item in the menu.

Impact

Severe accessibility violation. Keyboard users and screen-reader users cannot dismiss the menu without committing to an action. Touch users have no way to close it without navigation.

After pressing ESC and clicking outside, the kebab menu (top right) is still visible.
BUG-02 HIGH

Uncaught TypeError: r?.hasAttribute is not a function

Surface/assets — JavaScript runtime error
Caught byTC_BH_12_AssetsPageNoConsoleErrors

Description

A JavaScript TypeError fires 5 times per /assets page load, originating in the application bundle (index-3RELFuA2.js). Optional chaining (r?.) protects against null/undefined but not against wrong-type values.

Steps to reproduce

  1. Open browser DevTools (F12) Console tab.
  2. Clear the console.
  3. Navigate to https://acme.qa.egalvanic.ai/assets
  4. Wait 5 seconds for page to fully load.
  5. Observe the Console output.

Expected behavior

Zero JavaScript errors logged at SEVERE level on a healthy page.

Actual behavior

5 SEVERE-level console errors: "Uncaught TypeError: r?.hasAttribute is not a function" originating from index-3RELFuA2.js:270:219332. The page still renders due to React error boundaries swallowing the throw, but the error indicates code is calling .hasAttribute() on something that is not an Element (likely a Text node).

Impact

Indicates broken state in DOM-traversal logic. Likely fix: change "r?.hasAttribute(...)" to "r instanceof Element && r.hasAttribute(...)" so the call only fires on actual DOM Elements.

BUG-03 HIGH

Dashboard asset count does not match asset list count

Surface/dashboard/site-overview vs /assets
Caught byCriticalPathTestNG.testCP_DI_001_AssetCountMatchesDashboard

Description

The Site Overview dashboard reports an asset count that does not match the underlying asset list. This indicates either stale cache, tenant-isolation breakage, or different data sources between the dashboard widget and the module page.

Steps to reproduce

  1. Log in to https://acme.qa.egalvanic.ai
  2. Navigate to Site Overview / Dashboard.
  3. Note the asset count shown in the KPI card.
  4. Navigate to /assets module.
  5. Note the total count shown in the pagination footer ("of N").

Expected behavior

The two counts match exactly (single source of truth).

Actual behavior

Dashboard reports 1,953 assets; the /assets grid reports 8,429 (delta of +6,476).

Impact

Customer-visible data integrity issue. Users cannot trust either number. Likely indicates dashboard widget queries a different endpoint or applies a filter/scope the list page does not.

Failure screenshot from automated cross-check.
BUG-04 HIGH

Dashboard task count does not match Tasks module

Surface/dashboard vs /tasks
Caught byCriticalPathTestNG.testCP_DI_002_TaskCountMatchesDashboard

Description

Dashboard reports pending tasks but the Tasks module shows zero. Same data-integrity class as BUG-03.

Steps to reproduce

  1. Log in to https://acme.qa.egalvanic.ai
  2. Navigate to Site Overview / Dashboard.
  3. Note the "Pending Tasks" KPI count.
  4. Navigate to /tasks module.
  5. Note the total count shown.

Expected behavior

Dashboard count matches Tasks module count.

Actual behavior

Dashboard reports 147 pending tasks; Tasks module reports 0.

Impact

Customer cannot reconcile task counts. Workflow ambiguity.

Cross-check failure: dashboard 147 vs module 0.
BUG-05 HIGH

Dashboard issue count does not match Issues module

Surface/dashboard vs /issues
Caught byCriticalPathTestNG.testCP_DI_003_IssueCountMatchesDashboard

Description

Same data-integrity class as BUG-03 / BUG-04.

Steps to reproduce

  1. Log in to https://acme.qa.egalvanic.ai
  2. Navigate to Site Overview / Dashboard.
  3. Note the "Unresolved Issues" KPI count.
  4. Navigate to /issues module.
  5. Note the total count shown.

Expected behavior

Dashboard count matches Issues module count.

Actual behavior

Dashboard reports 266 unresolved issues; Issues grid reports 0.

Impact

Customer-visible data inconsistency.

Cross-check failure: dashboard 266 vs grid 0.
BUG-06 HIGH

Asset grid is empty after refresh

Surface/assets — state recovery on refresh
Caught byCriticalPathTestNG.testCP_DI_005_AssetDataSurvivesRefresh

Description

After navigating to /assets and refreshing the page (F5), the grid has no clickable rows.

Steps to reproduce

  1. Log in to https://acme.qa.egalvanic.ai
  2. Navigate to /assets.
  3. Wait for the asset grid to render with rows.
  4. Press F5 or refresh the browser.
  5. Wait 5+ seconds for re-render.

Expected behavior

Asset grid re-fetches and displays the same rows.

Actual behavior

Grid renders empty — no clickable rows are present after refresh.

Impact

Users cannot reliably refresh the page to get fresh data. F5 should be a safe operation.

Asset grid empty after refresh.
BUG-07 HIGH

Beamer widget URL leaks user PII (role / company / name) in query params

SurfaceBeamer notification widget URL
Caught byBugHuntGlobalTestNG.testBUG018_BeamerLeaksUserData

Description

The Beamer in-app notification widget URL contains user PII as query parameters: role, company, and name. These are visible in browser network tab and may be logged by Beamer's servers and any intermediate proxies.

Steps to reproduce

  1. Log in to https://acme.qa.egalvanic.ai
  2. Open browser DevTools Network tab.
  3. Filter for "beamer" requests.
  4. Inspect the request URL of any Beamer call.

Expected behavior

User identity (name, role, company) should be passed via secure POST body or HTTP-only cookie, not URL query parameters.

Actual behavior

URL contains query parameters revealing role=true, company=true, name=true (user identity leaked).

Impact

GDPR / privacy concern. URLs are routinely logged at proxies, CDNs, and analytics tools. User PII should never appear in URLs.

Beamer URL with user data exposed in query params.
BUG-08 MEDIUM

/api/auth/v2/me returns 401 on every page load

SurfaceAuthentication bootstrap
Caught byTC_BH_12_AssetsPageNoConsoleErrors (network monitor)

Description

On every page load, the request to /api/auth/v2/me returns HTTP 401. The app appears to recover via a fallback path, but the failed call pollutes browser console as a SEVERE error.

Steps to reproduce

  1. Open browser DevTools Network tab.
  2. Navigate to https://acme.qa.egalvanic.ai
  3. Filter for "auth/v2".
  4. Note the response status of /me.

Expected behavior

/api/auth/v2/me returns 200 OK with user info.

Actual behavior

Returns 401 Unauthorized. Likely a token-bootstrap race — the request fires before the authentication token is set in headers.

Impact

Console pollution; backend log noise; slow page boot due to retry path.

BUG-09 MEDIUM

/api/auth/v2/refresh returns 400 on every page load

SurfaceAuthentication refresh
Caught byTC_BH_12_AssetsPageNoConsoleErrors

Description

Token refresh endpoint returns HTTP 400 on every page load. Suggests an unconditional refresh attempt with malformed payload, or refresh fired before the refresh token is available.

Steps to reproduce

  1. Open DevTools Network tab.
  2. Navigate to https://acme.qa.egalvanic.ai
  3. Filter for "auth/v2/refresh".

Expected behavior

Refresh either returns 200 OK with new tokens, or is not called when not needed.

Actual behavior

Returns 400 Bad Request on every page load.

Impact

Wasted backend cycles; console pollution; potential security audit flag.

BUG-10 MEDIUM

PLuG (DevRev support widget) authentication initialization fails

Surface/assets — third-party PLuG widget
Caught byTC_BH_12_AssetsPageNoConsoleErrors

Description

PLuG (DevRev support chat widget) logs "Error initializing with authentication" three times per /assets page load.

Steps to reproduce

  1. Open DevTools Console tab.
  2. Navigate to https://acme.qa.egalvanic.ai/assets
  3. Wait for page to fully load.

Expected behavior

PLuG widget initializes silently or with a single info log.

Actual behavior

Three SEVERE-level errors: "[PLuG] Error initializing with authentication:
